taxonomy: Pleurothallopsis nemorosa is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This epiphytic orchid is a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The classification of Pleurothallopsis nemorosa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Pleurothallopsis nemorosa, also known as the Forest Pleurothallopsis, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallopsis nemorosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions.
